foundation.title: CHOICE
foundation.subtitle: Truth Values
foundation.content: C++ uses the 'bool' type for logic. It literally stores only True or False. If statements form the 'Choice Points' of every C++ game engine.
foundation.scenario: A Physical Switch. If the switch is 'ON' (bool is true), the motor runs. If it's 'OFF' (bool is false), everything stops.

examples.title: The Simple If
examples.explanation: Checking one single condition. If it's false, the code inside the block is skipped.

examples.title: The Boolean Check
examples.explanation: Using a bool variable to control the flow of a program.

examples.title: The Comparison
examples.explanation: Checking two values for equality using '==' or difference with '!='.

foundation.keyPoint: C++ uses bool for literal True/False.

foundation.title: ELSE_IF
foundation.subtitle: Decision Chains
foundation.content: For more than two choices, C++ uses 'else if'. It is a chain of checks that the CPU evaluates one-by-one until it finds a match.
foundation.scenario: A Skill Priority System. IF (Health below 10) { Heal } ELSE IF (Mana above 20) { Magic } ELSE { Attack }.

examples.title: The Chain
examples.explanation: Ranking results into multiple logical categories.
examples.code: if (x > 9) { high(); } else if (x > 5) { mid(); } else { low(); }

examples.title: Input Mapping
examples.explanation: Deciding which command to run based on a string mapping.

examples.title: Safe Default
examples.explanation: Defining a baseline action if all other checks fail.

foundation.keyPoint: Once a True one is found, the rest of the chain is ignored.

foundation.title: AND_OR
foundation.subtitle: Complex Logic
foundation.content: Combine checks with '&&' (AND) and '||' (OR). This allows you to create high-level tactical conditions for your AI characters.
foundation.scenario: A Launch Protocol. IF (Key 1 AND Key 2 AND Weather is Clear) THEN Launch Rocket. Failing just ONE part stops the countdown.

examples.title: The Double AND
examples.explanation: The check ONLY passes if BOTH parts are true simultaneously.

examples.title: The Double OR
examples.explanation: The check passes if EITHER part is true (No need for both).

examples.title: Logical Flip with !
examples.explanation: Using 'not' to check for a missing state. '!dead' means alive.
